results.title: Skills-Coach: An Automated Framework for Agent Skill Self-Evolution
results.content: the most aggressive relative leap. They started out failing most of the time at around 27% and jumped to almost 83%. What this tells us is that the autofixer and those sequential code optimization paths are incredibly effective at debugging real technical execution. It's not just tweaking natural language prompts, it's genuinely fixing code. The evaluation summary metrics isolate exactly where the heavy lifting is happening. Just looking at the advanced task score reveals the most critical delta. For complex edge case scenarios, the original score was failing at 375. After optimization, a perfect 1.0. And maybe the best part, the regression count is absolutely zero. It expanded the capability boundaries perfectly without breaking any of the previously functioning core features. That is [...] structural flaws, apps and input validation logic, missing credential file paths, undocumented dependencies and the parameters. It essentially creates a highly deterministic blueprint. So when the code optimization pathway steps in, it knows exactly what to fix. Okay, let's look at the numbers because the quantitative results here are phenomenal. The researchers tested this on Skill X, a comprehensive benchmark data set of 48 diverse skills pulled from major platforms. Across the entire benchmark, the original baseline pass rate was a pretty dismal 33.59%. But after running through the skills coach framework, that optimized pass rate rocketed to 88.02%. That is a massive 54.43% absolute jump. It proves this framework doesn't just theorize, it delivers real robust execution reliability. So [...] -Coach is an innovative self-evolving framework developed to improve the functionality of large-scale language model (LLM)-based agents in an automated way. The system utilizes training-free GRPO algorithms to overcome the limitations of a fragmented technology ecosystem to optimize agents' command guidelines and code on their own. The framework consists of four core modules, from modules that generate advanced test cases, to four core modules that are responsible for optimization, execution comparison, and precision evaluation. In particular, it is designed to support both virtual and physical modes for flexible performance verification regardless of the environment. To verify this, the researchers built the Skill-X benchmark, which includes 48 different technologies, and the experiment

results.title: SkillCoach: Self-Evolving Rubrics for Enhancing Agentic Skill-Use
results.content: Introducing the SkillCoach framework that helps giant language model (LLM) agents effectively utilize complex technology libraries. The existing method of simply judging the success of the final result has the limitation of not being able to capture the problem of the agent accidentally getting the right answer or going through the wrong process. To address this, SkillCoach creates a self-evolving rubric that evaluates the agent's course of conduct in four dimensions: skill selection, procedural compliance, skill combination, and result reflection. According to research, the system significantly improves the agent's proficiency by accurately diagnosing the agent's potential failure points and selecting high-quality learning data. As a result, this framework ensures that agents operate [...] one, the skill use reliability problem. To really grasp the foundational issue here, we've got to look at the mechanics of outcome only filtering. When you rely purely on a final success metric, you are injecting massive noise into your training data. Notice how just relying on a final verifier completely masks critical trajectory flaws. I mean, agents operating in complex environments can easily pass via brute force trial and error. They'll pick completely irrelevant distractor skills, skip necessary computational steps, maybe hallucinate tool parameters, completely butcher the workflow logic, but still accidentally trigger a success state. Process supervision, on the other hand, strictly exposes these hidden failures. It tracks the operational footprint step by step, ensuring the model [...] As a result, this framework ensures that agents operate more reliably even in large environments, such as enterprise libraries.

Consequently, it achieves a closed-loop iterative refinement and autonomous evolution of skill capabilities without requiring continuous human intervention[yuan2024self]. results.title: Self-Evolving Rubrics for Evaluating and Enhancing Agentic Skill-Use
results.content:
We introduce SkillCoach, a self-evolving rubric framework for evaluating and enhancing agentic skill-use. SkillCoach derives skill-grounded process rubrics from real rollouts and evaluates trajectories along four dimensions: skill selection, skill following, skill composition, and skill-grounded reflection. It keeps the external verifier as a separate outcome signal, allowing process quality to be distinguished from accidental task success. The evolved rubrics further serve as process supervision for selecting high-quality training trajectories.
Experiments show that evolved rubrics substantially improve evaluation quality, expose failures hidden by final accuracy, and provide stronger supervision signals than outcome-only filtering for enhancing agentic skill-use. [...] We propose SkillCoach, the first self-evolving rubric framework for evaluating and enhancing agentic skill-use. We define agentic skill-use as a trajectory-level meta-ability with four dimensions: skill selection, skill following, skill composition, and skill-grounded reflection. The external verifier is kept separate, which allows us to distinguish reliable skill-use behavior from accidental verifier passing. The evolved rubrics are then used in two ways: they diagnose skill-use failures, and they select high-quality trajectories for supervised skill-use training. [...] SkillCoach is designed for two deployment realities. First, we focus on skill-dependent tasks, where strong agents struggle without the relevant skill but improve when the gold skill is available. This shifts our focus from evolving skills to evolving rubrics that check how skills are used. Second, we evaluate agents in distractor-augmented skill libraries, where gold skills are mixed with distractor skills, approximating enterprise repositories with overlapping workflows.

results.title: Paper page - Skills-Coach: A Self-Evolving Skill Optimizer via Training-Free GRPO
results.content:
We introduce Skills-Coach, a novel automated framework designed to significantly enhance the self-evolution of skills within Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents. Addressing the current fragmentation of the skill ecosystem, Skills-Coach explores the boundaries of skill capabilities, thereby facilitating the comprehensive competency coverage essential for intelligent applications. The framework comprises four core modules: a Diverse Task Generation Module that systematically creates a comprehensive test suite for various skills; a Lightweight Optimization Module dedicated to optimizing skill prompts and their corresponding code; a Comparative Execution Module facilitating the execution and evaluation of both original and optimized skills; and a Traceable Evaluation Module, which [...] We introduce Skills-Coach, a novel automated framework designed to significantly enhance the self-evolution of skills within Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents. Addressing the current fragmentation of the skill ecosystem, Skills-Coach explores the boundaries of skill capabilities, thereby facilitating the comprehensive competency coverage essential for intelligent applications. The framework comprises four core modules: a Diverse Task Generation Module that systematically creates a comprehensive test suite for various skills; a Lightweight Optimization Module dedicated to optimizing skill prompts and their corresponding code; a Comparative Execution Module facilitating the execution and evaluation of both original and optimized skills; and a Traceable Evaluation Module, which [...] Hugging Face's logo

# Skills-Coach: A Self-Evolving Skill Optimizer via Training-Free GRPO

## Abstract

Skills-Coach is an automated framework that enhances LLM-based agent skill evolution through four core modules for task generation, optimization, execution, and evaluation, validated on a benchmark of 48 diverse skills.
